text: Could I get a medium latte {{c1::to go}}, please?
explanation: to go is used when you want to take your food or drink out of the shop instead of consuming it there.
usage:
Pattern: Could I get a [size] [drink] to go, please?
  • Could I get a large Americano to go, please?
  • Could I get a small espresso to go, please?
spanish: ¿Me da un latte mediano para llevar, por favor?

text: I'd like to {{c1::substitute}} whole milk {{c1::with}} oat milk, please.
explanation: substitute A with B means to use B instead of A. This is the standard way to customize food or drink orders.
usage:
Pattern: substitute [original] with [alternative]
  • Can I substitute white bread with wheat bread?
  • I'd like to substitute fries with a side salad, please.
spanish: Me gustaría cambiar la leche entera por leche de avena, por favor.

text: Do you have any {{c1::sugar-free}} syrup options?
explanation: sugar-free means containing no sugar. Syrups are sweet liquid flavorings added to coffee (like vanilla or caramel).
usage:
Pattern: [adjective]-free (e.g., gluten-free, caffeine-free, tax-free)
  • Is this cookie gluten-free?
  • I'd prefer decaf, do you have caffeine-free coffee?

explanation: pay by card means to pay using a credit or debit card rather than cash.
usage:
Pattern: pay by [method] (e.g., pay by card, pay by phone, pay by check) but pay in cash
  • You can pay by phone using Apple Pay.
  • Is it cheaper if I pay in cash?

text: I would like to order a large cold brew with a {{c1::splash of}} oat milk.
explanation: A 'splash of' is an informal measurement meaning a very small amount of liquid (typically milk, cream, or water) added to a drink.
usage:
Pattern: splash of [liquid]
  • Just a splash of milk in my tea, please.
  • He likes his whiskey with a splash of soda.
spanish: Me gustaría pedir un café extraído en frío grande con un chorrito de leche de avena.

text: Do you have any {{c1::dairy-free}} options for the latte?
explanation: 'Dairy-free' refers to foods or beverages that contain no milk or milk derivatives, suitable for lactose-intolerant or vegan customers.
usage:
Pattern: dairy-free milk / plant-based milk
  • Soy and almond milk are common dairy-free alternatives.
  • Is this caramel syrup dairy-free?
spanish: ¿Tienen alguna opción sin lácteos para el café con leche?

text: I'll have a double espresso, and can I get that {{c1::sweetened with}} honey?
explanation: To get something 'sweetened with' means to add a specific sweetener (like honey, sugar, syrup, or stevia) to a drink or food.
usage:
Pattern: sweetened with [sweetener]
  • I prefer my tea sweetened with raw sugar.
  • This lemonade is sweetened with agave nectar.
spanish: Tomaré un espresso doble, ¿y me lo pueden dar endulzado con miel?

text: Is there a password for the {{c1::guest Wi-Fi}} here?
explanation: 'Guest Wi-Fi' is a secondary wireless network provided by businesses for clients and visitors, keeping the internal network secure.
usage:
Pattern: guest Wi-Fi / public network
  • The guest Wi-Fi is free for customers who buy a drink.
  • Ask the barista for the guest Wi-Fi password.
spanish: ¿Hay contraseña para el Wi-Fi para invitados aquí?

text: I'd like to buy a bag of whole bean coffee, could you {{c1::grind it for}} drip?
explanation: To 'grind it for [method]' means to crush whole coffee beans into the specific particle size required for a brewing method (e.g. drip, espresso, french press).
usage:
Pattern: grind [coffee] for [brewing method]
  • Could you grind these beans for a French press?
  • I grind my own coffee beans for espresso every morning.
spanish: Me gustaría comprar una bolsa de café en grano entero, ¿podrían molerlo para cafetera de filtro?

text: Can I get a pastry to go, and please {{c1::warm it up}} for me?
explanation: To 'warm up' something means to make it warm or hot, usually by putting it in a microwave, oven, or toaster.
usage:
Pattern: warm [something] up / heat up
  • Would you like me to warm up your croissant?
  • I need to warm up my lunch in the microwave.
spanish: ¿Me da una factura/pan dulce para llevar, y por favor me la calienta?
